title: Lumen GM Hub — Full Product Specification v3
content:
# Lumen GM Hub — Complete Product Specification (v3)

**Date:** March 10, 2026
**Supersedes:** v2 (March 7, 2026)

## Changelog from v2

- **Audio architecture:** Replaced Discord loopback + virtual audio device + pyannote diarization with a built-in VoIP client delivering per-speaker audio streams. Speaker attribution is now deterministic, not probabilistic.
- **Removed dependencies:** pyannote.audio, VB-Cable (virtual audio device), WASAPI loopback, voice enrollment (for attribution purposes).
- **Added:** Built-in WebRTC voice client (browser-based for players, integrated in Hub for GM).
- **Business model:** Moved from flat monthly subscription to a two-tier model (local + cloud) with different pricing and infrastructure profiles.
- **VRAM budget:** Dropping pyannote frees ~1.0–1.5 GB, shifting the Whisper model sweet spot from `small` to `medium` on 8GB cards, with `large-v3` now testable.
- **Infrastructure:** Added lightweight signaling server + STUN/TURN for VoIP connectivity. Cloud tier adds media server + GPU compute.
- **Onboarding:** Simplified significantly — no virtual audio device installation, no audio routing configuration.

---

## 1. Product Summary

Lumen GM Hub is a local-first companion app for PF2e games run in Foundry VTT (v13) that:

- Provides a **built-in VoIP voice client** for game sessions, capturing each player's audio as an independent stream. Players join via a browser link — no software installation required.
- **Transcribes speech in real time** using local Whisper (cloud transcription available on premium tier), with speaker attribution solved at the capture layer — each audio stream is a known player.
- Produces GM-facing **suggestions** (~3 high-quality prompts per scene) tuned to campaign tone, pacing, genre, and a persistent control panel with per-session overrides.
- Maintains **campaign-long memory** that grows from suggestions the GM explicitly "uses & saves", post-session GM additions, or automatic transcript extraction (configurable per campaign).
- **Detects conflicts** between spoken canon and stored memory; alerts are configurable per conflict type with per-type interrupt thresholds.
- **Writes session notes** to Foundry Journals; applies GM-approved sheet updates (HP/conditions) via granular write permission toggles the GM controls at their own pace.
- Delivers a **post-session mode** with written summary, NPC/faction status changes, unresolved threads, next-session prep suggestions, and GM feedback on suggestion quality.
- Ships with a **Campaign Setup Wizard** supporting note import from plain text/markdown, Obsidian vaults, World Anvil, and PDF/Word docs.
- Surfaces a **read-only Campaign Ledger** snapshot for players inside Foundry.

### Non-goals (v1)

- No long-term audio recording to disk.
- No automatic sheet changes without GM approval.
- PF2e only.
- No managed API keys (BYO keys for OpenAI / Anthropic).
- No macOS support at launch (Windows first).
- No video, screen share, or text chat in the VoIP client.
- No Discord integration or fallback audio path.

---

## 2. System Scope

| Component | Detail |
|---|---|
| Foundry | v13 (same machine as GM Hub) |
| System | PF2e only (open rules + Foundry compendiums) |
| Voice | Built-in WebRTC VoIP client. Players join via browser; GM captures per-stream audio locally |
| Networking | Hub UI and Foundry connector on localhost (`127.0.0.1`). VoIP signaling via Lumen cloud relay. Audio streams are peer-to-peer (P2P) with TURN fallback for restricted networks |
| Business model | Two-tier subscription (see Section 15). BYO keys for OpenAI / Anthropic (suggestion engine) |
| Maintenance | Monthly releases |
| Platform | Windows 10/11 at launch; macOS later via Electron packaging |

---

## 3. Architecture

### 3.1 Components

**1. GM Hub (Electron app)**

Hosts local UI (separate window alongside Foundry) and WS API. Owns audio capture, transcription, suggestions, memory, conflicts, exports, encryption, licensing, and the GM-side VoIP endpoint.

**2. VoIP Client**

Built-in WebRTC voice client replacing the virtual audio device from v2.

- **GM side:** Integrated into the Hub Electron app. Captures GM's local mic as one stream; receives each player's audio as independent WebRTC streams.
- **Player side:** Lightweight browser-based client (HTML/JS). No installation required. Player opens a session link shared by the GM, grants mic permission, and connects.
- Audio only — no video, screen share, text chat, or channel system.
- Opus codec at 48kHz. Bitrate configurable in Hub settings (higher bitrate = better Whisper accuracy, more bandwidth).
- Optional RNNoise preprocessing for noisy player mics (lightweight, CPU-based).
- Connection via WebRTC with Lumen signaling server for ICE negotiation. Peer-to-peer audio where possible; TURN relay fallback for restricted NATs.

**3. Signaling Server (Lumen-hosted, lightweight)**

- Minimal WebSocket server facilitating WebRTC connection establishment between players and the GM's Hub.
- Handles ICE candidate exchange, session creation/teardown, and connection health monitoring.
- Never handles or receives audio data — once WebRTC connections are established, the signaling server is out of the audio path.
- Bundled STUN service for NAT traversal. TURN relay available for connections that can't establish P2P.
- Infrastructure cost: ~$10–20/month (flat, regardless of user count). Audio-only TURN bandwidth is minimal (~100 kbps per stream).

**4. Foundry v13 Module (free connector)**

- Adds "Lumen Hub" panel embedding Hub UI via iframe.
- Connects to Hub WS and streams Foundry state (scene / combat / party / actors).
- Executes approved write commands (Journal + HP/conditions), gated by per-permission toggles.
- Serves read-only Campaign Ledger snapshot to players.

### 3.2 Local Endpoints

| Endpoint | Address |
|---|---|
| Hub UI | `http://127.0.0.1:17171` |
| Hub WS (Foundry connector) | `ws://127.0.0.1:17172` |
| VoIP signaling (local) | Managed internally by Electron; no exposed port for players (they connect via the signaling server) |

### 3.3 Audio Pipeline

The VoIP-first architecture fundamentally simplifies the audio pipeline by solving speaker attribution at the capture layer.

```
Player A ──WebRTC (P2P)──→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player A]: "text"
Player B ──WebRTC (P2P)──→   GM's Machine (Lumen Hub)   ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player B]: "text"
Player C ──WebRTC (P2P)──→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player C]: "text"
GM Mic   ──local capture──→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [GM]: "text"
                                                                  │
                                                        Chronological merge
                                                                  │
                                                        Attributed transcript
```

**Pipeline stages per stream:**

1. **Capture layer:** Each player's audio arrives as an independent WebRTC stream (Opus-encoded). GM's mic captured locally via system audio API. Each stream is tagged with the player's identity at capture time.
2. **VAD layer:** Silero VAD runs per-stream in real time; pauses transcription chunking during non-speech energy.
3. **Transcription layer:** `faster-whisper` (CTranslate2 backend) processes each stream's speech segments. Architecture: single shared Whisper model instance processing a queued segment pool from all streams (preferred for VRAM efficiency), or per-stream instances if latency requires it (determined by PoC benchmarking).
4. **Merge layer:** Transcribed, speaker-labeled segments from all streams are merged into a single chronological transcript with timestamp alignment. Simultaneous speech from different speakers produces parallel transcript entries.
5. **Confidence scoring:** Composite score from ASR confidence, context completeness (party/scene/actor mapping + memory retrieval sufficiency), and contradiction detector certainty. Low confidence pauses suggestions and surfaces the segment for GM review. Speaker attribution confidence is no longer a scoring factor — it is inherently ~100% with per-stream capture.

**Target transcription latency:** < 3 seconds post-segment finalize (local tier). < 2 seconds (cloud tier, more powerful GPU).

### 3.4 What Was Removed from v2

| v2 Component | Reason for Removal |
|---|---|
| Virtual audio device (VB-Cable) | Replaced by built-in VoIP — no loopback capture needed |
| WASAPI loopback capture | Replaced by direct WebRTC stream capture |
| pyannote.audio diarization | Speaker attribution solved at capture layer — each stream is a known player |
| Voice enrollment | No longer required for attribution (optional future feature for voice profiles) |
| Speaker attribution confidence scoring | Attribution is deterministic, not probabilistic |

### 3.5 VRAM Budget (Local Tier — RTX 3070 Reference)

Without pyannote, Whisper is the only GPU-resident model. The VRAM budget simplifies significantly.

| Whisper Model | VRAM (approx) | Expected Accuracy (English) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tiny` | ~0.4 GB | Low | Not viable for production |
| `base` | ~0.5 GB | Moderate | Struggles with accents |
| `small` | ~1.0 GB | Good | Viable minimum for 6GB cards |
| `medium` | ~2.5 GB | Very good | **Recommended for 8GB cards** |
| `large-v3` | ~3.5–4.5 GB | Best available | Viable on 8GB; optimal on 12GB+ |

**Total pipeline VRAM (`medium` path):** ~3.1–4.0 GB including VAD + CUDA overhead. Leaves ~4–5 GB headroom on an 8GB card for Foundry, browser, and system use.

| Spec Tier | GPU VRAM | Whisper Model | Expected Experience |
|---|---|---|---|
| Minimum | 6 GB | `small` | Functional, occasional accuracy gaps |
| Recommended | 8 GB | `medium` | Smooth, good accuracy |
| Optimal | 12+ GB | `large-v3` | Best accuracy, full headroom |

Cloud tier (Section 15) eliminates the GPU requirement entirely — transcription runs on Lumen's servers.

---

## 4. Campaign Setup Wizard

Run on first pairing per Foundry world, or manually from campaign settings.

### 4.1 Wizard Steps

**Step 1 — Campaign Identity**

- Campaign name, setting summary (free text).
- Genre tags (multi-select): Horror, Political Intrigue, Dungeon Crawl, Exploration, Mystery, Combat-heavy, Social-heavy, Custom.

**Step 2 — Tone & Pacing**

- Tone dial: Gritty/Dark ←→ Heroic/Light (slider, 5 points).
- Pacing preference: Slow Burn ←→ Action-Forward (slider, 5 points).
- Both settings feed directly into suggestion weighting.

**Step 3 — Table Setup**

- Player count.
- Player experience level (new to TTRPG / casual / experienced / veteran).
- Player display names for transcript labeling (pre-populated from Foundry party roster if available; editable by GM).

**Step 4 — Lines & Veils**

- Hard lines (content never to be invoked or suggested).
- Soft veils (content handled off-screen or fade-to-black).
- Stored locally; Lumen will never generate suggestions that reference flagged content.

**Step 5 — Note Import (optional)**

- Import existing campaign notes to pre-seed Campaign Memory and the Ledger.
- Supported formats: Plain text / Markdown, Obsidian vault (folder select), World Anvil (API key + world ID), PDF, Word (.docx).
- Lumen parses and maps imported content to Ledger sections (NPCs, Locations, Factions, Plot Threads, etc.) with a review/approval step before committing.
- Eliminates cold start problem; suggestions are relevant from session one.

**Step 6 — Review & Confirm**

- Summary of all wizard inputs with inline edit links per step.
- "Launch Campaign" creates the Ledger, seeds memory, and completes pairing.

---

## 5. Core UX Flows

### 5.1 First-run & Pairing

1. GM installs Lumen Hub (Electron app) and launches it.
2. Foundry module connects to Hub WS and requests pairing.
3. Hub displays pairing code; GM enters in Foundry panel once.
4. Hub stores pairing token locally; Foundry stores in world settings.
5. Campaign Setup Wizard launches automatically on first pairing per world.

No virtual audio device installation. No audio routing configuration. The VoIP client is built into the Hub — voice setup happens at session start, not during installation.

### 5.2 Auto-create Campaign Ledger

After wizard completion, Hub issues `journal.ensureLedger` to Foundry. Foundry creates folder "Lumen (Auto)" and journal "Campaign Ledger" if missing. Ledger uses Template Version 1 (see Section 18).

### 5.3 Session Flow

1. GM clicks "Start Session" in Hub. Hub generates a session link.
2. GM shares the session link with players (paste into Discord, text, email — whatever the group uses for coordination).
3. Players open the link in their browser. Minimal UI: grant mic permission, see connected players, join voice.
4. GM selects/overrides Active Party (Party Journal) and optionally sets Narrative Scene.
5. Audio capture begins — each player's stream captured independently. VAD and transcription run continuously.
6. Live transcript scrolls in the Hub with speaker labels and timestamps.
7. Suggestions surface in the Suggestion Queue with context cadence (see Section 7).
8. GM triages suggestions via the full action suite (see Section 7.3).
9. Conflict alerts fire per per-type interrupt threshold configuration (see Section 9).
10. "End Session" disconnects all VoIP streams and transitions to Post-Session Mode (see Section 11).

### 5.4 Scene Cadence

Hybrid scene model:

- Foundry Scene changes detected automatically.
- Narrative scenes: GM button/hotkey; Hub can suggest "New scene?" based on topic shift + inactivity.

---

## 6. Parties, Players, Characters

One Foundry World = one campaign. Multiple Parties inside world are supported via Party Journals. Players can have multiple characters; ambiguous character mapping triggers a prompt (but speaker identity is always known).

Party roster pre-populated from Foundry actor data.

### Speaker Attribution

Speaker attribution is **deterministic** in the VoIP-first architecture. Each WebRTC stream is a known player. No probabilistic diarization, no enrollment required.

| Layer | Mechanism |
|---|---|
| Stream identity | Each WebRTC connection is associated with a player name (set at join time) |
| Foundry mapping | Player names mapped to Foundry actor(s) via Party Journal |
| Multi-character | If a player has multiple characters, GM sets default; Hub prompts on ambiguity |
| GM correction | Click any transcript segment to reassign speaker label (edge case cleanup) |

### OOC Handling

- Manual OOC toggle in Hub UI (per-player or global).
- Heuristic prompt suggests marking OOC when off-topic; GM confirms.
- OOC segments excluded from memory and suggestion context.

---

## 7. Suggestions System

### 7.1 Categories (rotated based on context)

- Dialogue beats
- Complications / interruptions
- Investigation prompts / clue pointing
- Mood dressing / scene setting
- Spotlight balancing
- Rules reminders (GM can override; PF2e compendium-sourced)
- Encounter tuning ideas
- Relationship drama / faction pressure
- Loot / treasure ideas
- Session steering & engagement steering

### 7.2 Suggestion Control Panel

**Persistent baseline (Campaign Settings)**

- Tone dial (Gritty ←→ Heroic): tunes suggestion register.
- Pacing dial (Slow Burn ←→ Action-Forward): biases category rotation.
- Category enable/disable toggles.
- Suggestion frequency target (suggestions per scene).
- Rules reminder verbosity (brief / detailed / off).

**Per-session overrides (Pre-session panel)**

- All baseline settings overridable for the current session only.
- "Reset to baseline" button.
- Accessible before session start and during session from settings drawer.

### 7.3 Suggestion Triage Actions

Each suggestion card in the queue exposes:

| Action | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Use Now | Apply immediately; prompts "Use & Save" to distill to Campaign Memory |
| Edit Before Applying | Opens inline editor; GM modifies text then applies |
| Save for Post-Session | Defers to post-session review queue |
| Flag for Conflict Check | Sends to conflict detector for manual review |
| Dismiss | Removes from queue; optionally logged for feedback |

### GM Feedback / Rating

Optional thumbs up/down + free text on each suggestion after use or dismiss. Ratings stored per campaign; used to tune future suggestion relevance within that campaign's context.

---

## 8. Write Permissions

All write permissions are granular toggles in Campaign Settings. No unlock progression — GM enables what they want at their own pace, in any order.

| Permission | What it enables |
|---|---|
| Journal: Append | Lumen can write to Campaign Ledger and session journals |
| Actor: HP Delta | Lumen can apply HP changes to actor sheets |
| Actor: Add Condition | Lumen can add conditions to actors |
| Actor: Remove Condition | Lumen can remove conditions from actors |

All writes are explicit Apply / Edit / Dismiss actions initiated by the GM. No automatic writes under any circumstances. Write permissions respect the GM user requirement in Foundry (Lumen only writes as the GM-authenticated user).

---

## 9. Conflict Detection

### 9.1 Conflict Types (v1)

- Timeline / date contradictions
- NPC status contradictions (alive vs. dead vs. missing)
- Location contradictions
- Loot / money continuity
- Relationship contradictions between characters
- Contradictions with imported GM notes

### 9.2 Per-type Interrupt Thresholds

Each conflict type has an independently configurable interrupt behavior:

- **Interrupt immediately** — alert fires mid-sentence.
- **Gentle ping** — non-blocking notification; defers to next natural break.
- **Queue silently** — accumulates in conflict review queue; no mid-session alert.

Defaults are pre-set based on genre tags from the wizard (e.g., horror campaigns default NPC status to "interrupt immediately"; dungeon crawl defaults loot to "queue silently").

### 9.3 Conflict Resolution

**In-session:** Per-type threshold fires alert. High-impact alerts defer automatically during active combat.

**End-of-session conflict review queue:**

GM sees: conflict description, prior canon, new statement, source timestamps, **speaker attribution** (which player said the conflicting statement — always accurate with per-stream capture).

Actions: Canonize new truth / Keep prior canon / Mark as intentional retcon.

Canon changes update Current Truth in Ledger and append to Retcons section.

---

## 10. Campaign Memory

### 10.1 Population Methods (per-campaign preference)

Configurable independently; any combination can be active:

- **Use & Save** — GM explicitly distills a used suggestion to memory.
- **Post-session additions** — GM reviews and approves additions after session.
- **Automatic from transcript** — Hub extracts candidate facts from transcript; GM curates in post-session review.

### 10.2 Memory Structure

Memory maps to Ledger sections: NPCs, Locations, Factions, Plot Threads, Clues, Items & Loot, House Rules, Canon Changes.

Each memory entry carries:

- Source session + timestamp
- **Speaker** (which player/GM contributed the information — always accurate)
- Confidence level
- GM approval status
- Linked Ledger entry (if applicable)

---

## 11. Post-Session Mode

Triggered by GM clicking "End Session". VoIP connections are closed. Distinct UI mode with its own flow.

### 11.1 Post-Session Report Contents

- **Written summary** — narrative summary of session events in GM's campaign tone.
- **NPC / faction status changes** — deltas from session start to end.
- **Unresolved threads & cliffhangers** — open plot threads flagged during session.
- **Prep suggestions for next session** — 3–5 suggested directions based on current memory state.
- **GM feedback** — star rating + free text on overall suggestion quality for the session.

### 11.2 Post-Session Review Queue

- Conflict review (see Section 9.3).
- Memory additions for approval (if automatic extraction is enabled).
- Deferred suggestions from "Save for Post-Session" triage action.
- Proposed Ledger updates for GM approval before writing.

---

## 12. Audio & Storage Policy

- No long-term audio saved by default (applies to both local and cloud tiers).
- Rolling RAM buffer (60 seconds) per stream.
- Optional encrypted "clip last 30s" export for diagnostics only, on explicit GM action.
- Local DB and exports encrypted at rest.
- Keys stored via OS keychain where available + encrypted fallback.
- Auto-archive on inactivity (N months configurable); 30-day warning via desktop notification.
- Archive is non-destructive and reversible.

**Cloud tier additional policy:** Audio streams processed in real-time memory on Lumen servers. Never written to disk, never stored, never used for model training. Audio is discarded immediately after transcription. Transcript text (not audio) relayed to GM's Hub.

---

## 13. Multiple Campaign Support

- Multiple campaigns supported; one active at a time.
- Campaign switcher in Hub sidebar; switching requires ending active session.
- Inactive campaigns retain full memory, ledger, and session history.
- Auto-archive applies per campaign independently.

---

## 14. Data Portability

| Feature | Details |
|---|---|
| Export campaign memory | Markdown or PDF; includes full Ledger, session summaries, and memory entries |
| Transfer campaign between machines | Full encrypted backup/restore bundle |
| Player read-only snapshot | Served via Foundry module; always reflects current Ledger state; GM controls which sections are visible to players |

---

## 15. Licensing & Subscription

### Two-Tier Model

#### Tier 1 — Local

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| Audio routing | WebRTC streams from players → GM's machine (P2P) |
| Transcription | Local `faster-whisper` on GM's GPU |
| Whisper model | Constrained by GM's VRAM |
| Server dependency | Signaling + STUN/TURN only |
| Privacy | Audio never leaves the GM's machine |
| Target user | Power users, privacy-conscious GMs, mid-range+ NVIDIA GPU |
| Pricing | Free, or low flat fee ($3–5/month) to cover signaling infrastructure |

#### Tier 2 — Cloud (Premium)

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| Audio routing | WebRTC streams from players → Lumen cloud media server |
| Transcription | Cloud Whisper (`large-v3` or best available) on GPU instances; transcript text sent to GM's Hub |
| Whisper model | Not constrained by GM hardware |
| Server dependency | Media server + GPU compute + signaling |
| Privacy | Audio processed in real-time memory, never stored, encrypted in transit |
| Target user | Laptop GMs, Mac users, AMD GPU users, anyone wanting best accuracy without hardware investment |
| Pricing | $12–20/month (covers GPU compute costs) |

#### Shared Across Tiers

- Same Hub UI, suggestion engine, memory system, conflict detection, Foundry integration.
- Same VoIP client — players don't know or care which tier the GM uses.
- Same Campaign Ledger, post-session reports, wizard, templates.
- GM can switch tiers at any time.

### Subscription Terms

- Online activation required.
- 7-day offline grace period (local tier only — cloud tier requires connectivity by definition).
- After grace, Viewer Mode: access past data; no new sessions/suggestions; Foundry connector read-only.
- Foundry module remains installable and can connect; premium features require active subscription.

### Email Notifications (only)

- Welcome / goodbye (subscription lifecycle).
- Archive warning: "No activity for N months; will archive in N days."
- No transcript or memory content is ever emailed.

---

## 16. Providers (v1)

- OpenAI + Anthropic only (for suggestion engine — BYO keys).
- Cost caps, rate limits, simple defaults, and advanced tuning panel.
- Transcription: Local `faster-whisper` (Tier 1) or Lumen cloud Whisper (Tier 2). No user-managed transcription API keys required.

---

## 17. Onboarding

- **In-app tutorial flow** — step-by-step guided tour on first launch covering VoIP setup, Foundry pairing, wizard, and session flow.
- **Sample campaign** — pre-loaded demo campaign to explore features without connecting a live Foundry world. Includes example memory entries, suggestions, and a mock post-session report.
- **VoIP test mode** — "Test your mic" flow where the GM can verify their own audio captures and transcribes correctly before inviting players.
- **Tooltips & contextual help** — inline on every major UI element; expandable "why does this matter?" explanations for non-technical GMs.
- **Community Discord** — linked from Help menu; primary support channel.

---

## 18. UI

### 18.1 Session Hub Window

Separate window alongside Foundry during sessions. Panels visible simultaneously:

- **Live Transcript Feed** — scrolling real-time transcript with speaker labels and color coding per player. Click any segment to edit speaker label or flag content.
- **Suggestion Queue** — active suggestion cards with full triage action suite.
- **Active Conflict Alerts** — per-type alert banners with resolution shortcuts.
- **Campaign Memory Browser** — searchable memory entries; expand to see source sessions.
- **Session Timeline / History** — chronological event log for the current session.
- **VoIP Status Panel** — connected players, connection quality indicators, per-player mute/transcription toggles, session link for sharing.

### 18.2 Player Voice Client (Browser)

Minimal browser-based UI opened via session link:

- Connected players list with speaking indicators.
- Mute/unmute toggle.
- Volume control (for what the player hears from others).
- Connection quality indicator.
- "Leave Session" button.
- No access to transcript, suggestions, memory, or any GM-facing features.

### 18.3 Keyboard Support

Full keyboard shortcut support alongside mouse-driven UI. All primary triage actions (use, edit, save, flag, dismiss) have configurable hotkeys. Designed for GMs who cannot take hands off keyboard during active sessions.

### 18.4 UI Customization

- Dark mode / Light mode toggle.
- Font size / accessibility options (3 sizes).
- Compact / expanded layout modes (compact suitable for smaller secondary monitors).
- Color coding by suggestion category (configurable color per category).
- Color coding by player/speaker (configurable in session settings).

---

## 19. Distribution & Updates

| Component | Channel |
|---|---|
| Foundry module (connector) | Foundry package listing |
| GM Hub (Electron app) | Direct download from Lumen website |
| Player voice client | No distribution — browser-based, accessed via session link |
| Updates | Built-in updater in Hub (can be disabled). Foundry module updates via Foundry package manager |

Platform: Windows 10/11 first; macOS later via Electron packaging. macOS support is more viable in v3 architecture since the cloud tier eliminates the CUDA dependency — Mac GMs can use Tier 2 at launch.

---

## 20. WebSocket API Contract (Summary)

Hub WS server on localhost (`ws://127.0.0.1:17172`).

- Pairing flow produces pairing token stored in Foundry world settings.
- Foundry sends snapshots: scene / combat / party / actors.
- Hub sends approval-gated commands: journal writes + HP/conditions.
- Player read-only snapshot endpoint served via module; GM controls section visibility.

VoIP signaling is handled separately via the Lumen signaling server (not the local WS API). The local WS API is exclusively for Hub ↔ Foundry communication.

---

## 21. VoIP Technical Specification

### 21.1 Protocol & Codec

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Protocol | WebRTC (SRTP for media, DTLS for key exchange) |
| Audio codec | Opus |
| Sample rate | 48 kHz |
| Default bitrate | 64 kbps (configurable: 32–128 kbps) |
| Channels | Mono (per-player) |
| Encryption | DTLS-SRTP (mandatory, WebRTC default) |

### 21.2 Connection Architecture

**Local tier (P2P):**

```
Player Browser ──WebRTC──→ GM's Lumen Hub (Electron)
     ↑                            │
     │      ICE negotiation       │
     └──── Signaling Server ──────┘
```

- Players establish direct peer-to-peer connections to the GM's machine.
- Signaling server facilitates ICE candidate exchange only — never handles audio.
- If P2P fails (restrictive NAT/firewall), connection falls back to TURN relay.

**Cloud tier:**

```
Player Browser ──WebRTC──→ Lumen Media Server (LiveKit or similar)
                                  │
                           Per-stream audio
                                  │
                           GPU Whisper instances
                                  │
                           Transcript text (WebSocket)
                                  │
                           GM's Lumen Hub
```

- All WebRTC streams terminate at the cloud media server.
- Audio processed server-side; only transcript text reaches the GM's machine.

### 21.3 Signaling Server

- Lightweight WebSocket server hosted by Lumen.
- Handles: session creation, ICE candidate relay, connection health monitoring, player join/leave events.
- Does not handle: audio data, transcription, suggestion logic, memory, or any campaign data.
- Stateless beyond active sessions — no long-term data storage.
- Deployment: small VPS ($10–20/month flat cost). Single region acceptable for v1; multi-region if latency requires it.

### 21.4 NAT Traversal

| Method | Role |
|---|---|
| STUN | Discovers public IP/port for P2P connection. Uses Lumen-hosted or public STUN servers |
| TURN | Relay fallback when P2P is impossible (CGNAT, strict firewalls). Lumen-hosted coturn instance |

Expected TURN usage: 10–30% of connections in real-world conditions. Audio-only bandwidth is minimal (~100 kbps per stream), so TURN infrastructure costs remain low.

### 21.5 Session Lifecycle

1. GM clicks "Start Session" → Hub requests session creation from signaling server → receives session ID + joinable link.
2. GM shares link with players.
3. Player opens link → browser client connects to signaling server → ICE negotiation with GM's Hub (local tier) or media server (cloud tier) → WebRTC audio stream established.
4. Hub receives independent audio streams, one per player. Each stream tagged with player identity.
5. During session: signaling server monitors connection health. If a player disconnects, Hub marks them inactive; player can rejoin via the same link.
6. GM clicks "End Session" → Hub sends teardown signal → all WebRTC connections closed → signaling server cleans up session state.

---

## 22. Templates (Template Version 1)

### 22.1 Campaign Ledger Template

```markdown
# Campaign Ledger

## Parties
### Party: <Party Name> (Journal Link)
- Members: @Actor links
- Notes: …

## NPCs
### <NPC Name> — <Role/Tag>
- Status: Alive/Dead/Missing/Unknown
- Affiliation: <Faction/None>
- Relationships: …
- Current Truth: …
- Sources: Session links/timestamps

## Locations
### <Location Name> — <Tag>
- Region: …
- Description: …
- Current Truth: …
- Secrets (GM): …
- Sources: …

## Factions
### <Faction Name>
- Goals: …
- Known members: …
- Current Truth: …
- Sources: …

## Plot Threads
### <Thread Title> — (Open/Closed)
- Stakes: …
- Progress: …
- Next pressure point: …
- Sources: …

## Clues
### <Clue> — (Found/Used/Ignored)
- Points to: …
- Found in: …
- Sources: …

## Items & Loot
### <Item or Treasure Thread>
- Owner: …
- Status: …
- Notes: …
- Sources: …

## House Rules
### <Rule Title>
- Ruling: …
- When it applies: …
- Sources: …

## Canon Changes (Retcons)
### <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM> — <Short summary>
- Previous: …
- New: …
- Decision: Canonized new / Kept existing
- Trigger: Session link + timestamp
```

## Appendix A — Cloud Tier Infrastructure Detail

*This appendix covers production infrastructure for the cloud (Tier 2) subscription. It is not required for the local tier or the PoC.*

### Cloud Infrastructure Diagram

```
                Lumen Cloud
                ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                │  Signaling Server                     │
                │  Media Server (LiveKit or similar)     │ ← receives all WebRTC streams
                │  GPU Whisper Instances                 │ ← per-stream transcription
                │  Transcript relay → GM's Hub           │
                └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                          ▲                     │
                WebRTC streams (players)    Transcript text (WS)
                          │                     ▼
                Players                    GM's Machine
                                           ├── Lumen Hub (Electron)
                                           ├── Suggestion engine + memory (CPU only)
                                           └── Foundry VTT connector
```

### Key Components

- **Media server:** LiveKit (preferred) or similar WebRTC media server. Receives player streams, demuxes per-player audio, feeds to transcription pipeline.
- **GPU compute:** `faster-whisper` on cloud GPU instances (AWS g5.xlarge / A10G, RunPod, Modal). Runs `large-v3` for best accuracy regardless of GM hardware.
- **Transcript relay:** Transcribed, speaker-labeled text sent to GM's Hub via WebSocket. Only text crosses the network to the GM — no audio.
- **GM's machine:** Runs Hub UI, suggestion engine, memory, conflict detection, Foundry connector. CPU-only workload. No GPU required.

### Cost Modeling

| Variable | Estimate | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Avg session length | 3–4 hours | Typical PF2e session |
| Sessions per month per GM | 4–5 | Weekly game night |
| GPU compute per session-hour | $0.50–1.00 | Provider and model dependent |
| Compute cost per GM per month | $6–20 | Narrow with real benchmarks |
| Media server + signaling (base) | $20–50/month | Scales with concurrent sessions |
| Target subscription price | $12–20/month | Must cover compute + margin |

### Why Not Third-Party Hosting?

The Ventrilo/Foundry Forge model (ship server software, let hosting providers run it) works for pure voice servers, but Lumen's media server is inseparable from the transcription pipeline. Hosting providers would need GPU instances, which most game server providers don't offer. Third-party hosting may become viable as GPU hosting commoditizes, but adds complexity without clear benefit at launch.
